question: Given a square 2D integer array (matrix), write a program to compute and print its transpose.
driver: int main() { int N; std::cin >> N; std::vector<std::vector<int>> matrix(N, std::vector<int>(N)); for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) { for (int j = 0; j < N; ++j) { std::cin >> matrix[i][j]; } } std::vector<std::vector<int>> transposedMatrix(N, std::vector<int>(N)); for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) { for (int j = 0; j < N; ++j) { transposedMatrix[j][i] = matrix[i][j]; } } for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) { for (int j = 0; j < N; ++j) { std::cout << std::setw(4) << transposedMatrix[i][j]; } std::cout << std::endl; } return 0; }
errors: The inner loop for transposing uses the condition `j <= N` instead of `j < N`. This leads to accessing `transposedMatrix[N][i]` and `matrix[i][N]` when `j` equals `N`, which are out-of-bounds accesses for vectors of size `N`. This causes undefined behavior, potentially leading to crashes or memory corruption at runtime.
